updates.title: Anthropic keeps taking features away from Claude Pro, and I'm running out of reasons to defend it
updates.summary: XDA editorial cataloguing Anthropic's rolling removal of features from the $20/month Claude Pro plan: the April A/B test that stripped Claude Code from new subscribers, the ban on third-party tools like OpenClaw, and the May 13 announcement that Agent SDK and claude -p programmatic usage moves to a separate credit pool on June 15. The author argues that Pro used to offer a generous all-in-one deal and each incremental cut makes it harder to justify over the API or a local model. The piece lands just days before the June 15 billing change takes effect, giving it a strong 'now what?' hook for consumers.
updates.accuracy_notes: Editorial opinion piece; the factual claims (credit pool amounts, June 15 date, A/B test scope) are corroborated by Anthropic's own announcements and multiple third-party sources. Framing is intentionally critical — worth acknowledging as analysis, not neutral reporting. Publication date inferred from search-result signals; exact timestamp not independently verified.

updates.title: Anthropic's Claude subscriptions no longer include Agent SDK and claude -p usage
updates.summary: XDA news article covering Anthropic's May 13 announcement that, starting June 15, all programmatic Claude usage — Agent SDK, third-party apps built on it, and the claude -p CLI flag — will draw from a new dedicated monthly credit pool instead of the interactive subscription pool. Pro gets $20 in credits, Max 5x gets $100, Max 20x gets $200; once credits are exhausted, usage is billed at full API list rates with no subsidy. The article explains the practical impact for developers and indie builders running automated workflows on subscription plans.
updates.accuracy_notes: News report; confirmed by Anthropic's official announcement and corroborated by TechTimes, devtoolpicks.com, and codersera.com coverage of the same change. Credit pool amounts and June 15 effective date are primary-source verified. Date of XDA article is inferred, not confirmed by timestamp.

updates.title: Claude Platform Release Notes — June 2, 2026
updates.summary: Two API-level changes shipped on June 2: (1) The advisor tool now accepts a max_tokens parameter on the tool definition, letting developers cap how many tokens the advisor model generates per call — useful for reducing latency and output-token cost in long-horizon agentic workloads that don't need full-length advisor responses. (2) The Claude API will no longer bill for requests that return stop_reason: 'refusal' without producing any output tokens, removing the charge for zero-output safety refusals.
updates.accuracy_notes: Primary source — extracted directly from the official Anthropic platform release notes page. Both changes are clearly documented with links to updated reference pages. Entry dated June 2, 2026 (72 h before today's run); no entries exist for June 3-5 on this page.
